ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_notes: Clear text-based PDF, fully legible. ICC accredited chamber certificate with security code.

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_notes: Clear text-based PDF, fully legible. Single-page invoice from Volvo Car Corporation.

lc_check.lc_reference_text: Terms of Payment:Letter of credit 120 days
lc_check.lc_reference_location: Invoice, page 1, Terms of Payment field
lc_check.lc_activation_reasoning: The phrase 'Letter of credit' appears in the invoice's Terms of Payment field, NOT in the certificate. The LC-exception (section 2.2) requires the CERTIFICATE to contain an explicit LC reference with a specific LC number. The certificate does not contain any LC reference, LC number, or any of the designated bank-term labels ('L/C', 'Letter of Credit', 'Documentary Credit', 'LC No.', 'LC Number'). The invoice mentions 'Letter of credit 120 days' as a payment term, but this is not in the certificate. Therefore, the LC-exception is NOT activated. Verification proceeds according to all checkpoints in section 4.

search_results.context: Prominently displayed at the top of the invoice as the issuing company name, with VOLVO logo
Postal address block at bottom of invoice

document_pairing.method: Certificate references invoice number 462008187; invoice bears invoice number 462008187. Consignor 'Volvo car corporation' matches invoice issuer 'Volvo Car Corporation'.

checkpoints.certificate_value: Volvo car corporation, Box 24035, SE-400 22, GÖTEBORG, Sweden
checkpoints.invoice_value: Volvo Car Corporation, SE-405 31 Göteborg, SWEDEN (issuer in header and footer)

comparison_steps.description: Search for consignor name in invoice
comparison_steps.result: MATCH found directly. 'Volvo Car Corporation' appears prominently in invoice header and footer. Certificate says 'Volvo car corporation' which normalizes to 'Volvo Car Corporation' (case normalization).
Invoice footer explicitly states 'SWEDEN' as part of postal address. VAT reg no 'SE556074308901' has SE-prefix confirming Sweden. MATCH.

rules_applied.description: Koncernstruktur – consignor name found in invoice header
Country verification via explicit address and VAT prefix
General normalization – case normalization applied (car → Car)

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate consignor: 'Volvo car corporation, Box 24035, SE-400 22, GÖTEBORG, Sweden'. Invoice issuer: 'Volvo Car Corporation' (header) with postal address 'SE-405 31 Göteborg, SWEDEN' (footer). After case normalization, company names are identical: 'Volvo Car Corporation'. Country 'Sweden' is explicitly confirmed in invoice footer address and via SE-prefix in VAT number SE556074308901. The postal addresses differ slightly (Box 24035, SE-400 22 vs SE-405 31) but per rule 4.1.5, address differences do not in themselves cause MISMATCH — the decisive factors are company name and country, both of which match. MATCH per 4.1.0 and 4.1.2.1.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MISMATCH due to different postal codes (400 22 vs 405 31)
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: Per rule 4.1.5, differences in street address or postal code do not in themselves cause MISMATCH for consignor. Company name and country are the decisive factors.

checkpoints.certificate_value: CARIBBEAN VEHICLE ALLIANCE LLC, 1019 MARGINAL J F KENNEDY, 00920 SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico
checkpoints.invoice_value: Caribbean Vehicle Alliance LLC, Avenida Kennedy 9, 00920 SAN JUAN, PUERTO RICO (Customer - Payer field)

comparison_steps.description: Identify primary invoicing field in invoice
comparison_steps.result: Invoice has a 'Customer - Payer' field which functions as Invoice-To/Bill-To. This is the primary field per 4.2.0.2.
Certificate: 'CARIBBEAN VEHICLE ALLIANCE LLC' → normalized: 'Caribbean Vehicle Alliance LLC'. Invoice: 'Caribbean Vehicle Alliance LLC'. Exact match after case normalization. MATCH.
Certificate: 'Puerto Rico'. Invoice: 'PUERTO RICO'. After case normalization: identical. MATCH.

rules_applied.description: Priority order for consignee identification – Customer-Payer field used as primary invoicing field
Address differences (Marginal J F Kennedy vs Avenida Kennedy 9) do not affect MATCH – only company name and country are decisive

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate consignee: 'CARIBBEAN VEHICLE ALLIANCE LLC, 1019 MARGINAL J F KENNEDY, 00920 SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico'. Invoice Customer-Payer: 'Caribbean Vehicle Alliance LLC, Avenida Kennedy 9, 00920 SAN JUAN, PUERTO RICO'. Company name match is exact after case normalization. Country match: 'Puerto Rico' = 'PUERTO RICO' after case normalization. The street addresses differ ('1019 MARGINAL J F KENNEDY' vs 'Avenida Kennedy 9'), but per rule 4.2.4, address differences do not in themselves cause MISMATCH – the decisive factors are company name and country, both of which match. MATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MISMATCH due to different street addresses (1019 Marginal J F Kennedy vs Avenida Kennedy 9)
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: Per rule 4.2.4, differences in street address between certificate and invoice do not in themselves cause MISMATCH. Company name and country are the decisive factors, and both match.

checkpoints.certificate_value: 1 New Volvo vehicles / 1 New Volvo vehicle, VIN number: YV4L12UK5T2726637, Invoice: 462008187, Invoice date: 11.12.2025
checkpoints.invoice_value: 1 piece new passenger car Volvo XC40 536 L1 4B 0C1 31, Chassis No: 5360726637, Invoice No: 462008187, Date: 11.12.2025

comparison_steps.description: Check if article numbers are present in certificate
comparison_steps.result: Certificate does not contain conventional article numbers. The VIN number YV4L12UK5T2726637 is a vehicle identification number, not an article/part number. No article number priority match applies.

comparison_steps.step: 4.3.5 Priority 3 – Generic description with invoice reference
comparison_steps.description: Certificate contains invoice reference 462008187 and date 11.12.2025, with goods described as 'New Volvo vehicle'
comparison_steps.result: Certificate references invoice 462008187 dated 11.12.2025. Invoice confirms invoice number 462008187 and date 11.12.2025. Both match exactly. Certificate describes '1 New Volvo vehicle' and invoice describes '1 piece new passenger car Volvo XC40'. These are consistent – the certificate gives a general vehicle description while the invoice provides specific model details. The invoice content is consistent with the certificate's description.

comparison_steps.step: 4.3.3 Priority 2 – Text matching of identity-bearing designation
comparison_steps.description: Additional verification of goods description via text matching
comparison_steps.result: Certificate: 'New Volvo vehicle'. Invoice: '1 piece new passenger car Volvo XC40'. Applying rule 4.3.3.0 (vehicle designation equivalence): 'vehicle' and 'passenger car' are equivalent designations per the explicit rule. 'New' is present in both. 'Volvo' (brand) is present in both. The invoice adds 'XC40' model designation which is a non-identity-bearing addition per 4.3.4 (does not change product identity when the brand 'Volvo' and type 'vehicle/passenger car' already match). MATCH.

comparison_steps.description: Compare VIN in certificate with Chassis No in invoice
comparison_steps.result: Certificate VIN: YV4L12UK5T2726637 (17 characters). Invoice Chassis No: 5360726637 (10 characters). The invoice's chassis number '5360726637' does NOT constitute an exact suffix of the certificate's VIN 'YV4L12UK5T2726637'. Checking: the last 10 characters of the VIN are 'K5T2726637'. The invoice chassis number is '5360726637'. These are different strings. However, the last 6 digits of the VIN are '726637' and the last 6 digits of the chassis number are '726637' – partial overlap at the end. Per rule 4.3.3.0.1, if the invoice chassis number is an exact suffix of the certificate VIN → MANUAL_REVIEW. Here '5360726637' is NOT a suffix of 'YV4L12UK5T2726637'. However, this discrepancy does not affect the overall goods description result since the invoice reference match via 4.3.5 has already been established and the VIN/chassis number is supplementary identifying information, not the identity-bearing designation itself.

rules_applied.description: Generic goods description with invoice reference – certificate references invoice 462008187 dated 11.12.2025, both matching the invoice exactly
Vehicle designation equivalence – 'vehicle' and 'passenger car' treated as equivalent
Non-identity-bearing additions – XC40 model code in invoice is additional detail that does not change product identity
VIN/Chassis number comparison – partial overlap noted but invoice reference already established via 4.3.5
rules_applied.outcome: Not decisive – goods verification already satisfied via 4.3.5

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate goods: '1 New Volvo vehicle' with VIN YV4L12UK5T2726637, referencing invoice 462008187 dated 11.12.2025. Invoice goods: '1 piece new passenger car Volvo XC40', Chassis No 5360726637, Invoice No 462008187, Date 11.12.2025. Verification via Priority 3 (4.3.5): Certificate contains explicit invoice reference (462008187) and date (11.12.2025), both matching the invoice exactly. Certificate's description 'New Volvo vehicle' is consistent with invoice's '1 piece new passenger car Volvo XC40' – per 4.3.3.0, 'vehicle' and 'passenger car' are equivalent. Invoice content (a new Volvo car) is fully consistent with certificate description. The VIN/chassis number discrepancy (YV4L12UK5T2726637 vs 5360726637) is noted but does not override the positive verification via invoice reference. The goods description is verified as MATCH via rules 4.3.5 and 4.3.3.0.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MISMATCH due to VIN YV4L12UK5T2726637 vs Chassis No 5360726637 being different
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: The VIN/chassis number is supplementary identifying information. The goods description verification is primarily established via the invoice reference (4.3.5) and the identity-bearing designation match (4.3.3.0). The VIN/chassis discrepancy may reflect different format conventions (full VIN vs factory chassis number). The invoice reference and goods type match provide sufficient verification.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MISMATCH because certificate says 'vehicle' but invoice says 'passenger car'
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: Per rule 4.3.3.0, 'vehicle' and 'passenger car' are explicitly treated as equivalent designations for the same type of goods.

comparison_steps.description: Box 7 contains 'G/W1729KGS'. This is the verification-grounding quantity per rule 4.4.2.1.
comparison_steps.result: Box 7 value: G/W 1729 KGS. This means Gross Weight 1729 kilograms.

comparison_steps.description: Search for numeric value 1729 in invoice
comparison_steps.result: Invoice states 'Weight: 1.729 kg'. Applying thousands separator normalization (4.4.3.3): '1.729' with period as thousands separator = 1729. Both values are integers. The period in '1.729' is a thousands separator (the value 1.729 kg = 1.729 kilograms would be implausibly low for a car). After normalization: 1729 kg. Certificate: 1729 KGS. Values match exactly.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate specifies G/W (Gross Weight). Invoice states 'Weight: 1.729 kg' without explicit GW/NW category.
comparison_steps.result: Certificate has one weight value with explicit category G/W. Invoice has one weight value without explicit GW/NW. Per 4.4.2.2 point 2: certificate has one weight value, the numeric value can be identified in invoice, and the invoice does NOT contain a contradictory alternative weight value of another category. Therefore → MATCH.

rules_applied.description: Box 7 is the verification-grounding quantity field – G/W1729KGS
Thousands separator normalization – '1.729' normalized to 1729 (period as thousands separator for integer value)
Weight category – single weight value without contradictory alternative, point 2 applies
Direct identification – numeric value 1729 identified in invoice after normalization

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate box 7: 'G/W1729KGS' = Gross Weight 1729 kilograms. Invoice 'Weight: 1.729 kg'. The period in '1.729' is interpreted as a thousands separator (not decimal) per rule 4.4.3.3, since a weight of 1.729 kg (less than 2 kg) would be nonsensical for a car, and the value 1729 kg is consistent with a car's gross weight. After normalization: both values are 1729 KG. The certificate explicitly states G/W (Gross Weight), while the invoice states only 'Weight' without GW/NW. Per rule 4.4.2.2 point 2, when the certificate has a single weight value, the value matches the invoice, and the invoice contains no contradictory alternative weight → MATCH. Numeric values are identical: 1729 = 1729. MATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MANUAL_REVIEW because invoice does not specify GW/NW category
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: Per rule 4.4.2.2 point 2: when certificate has a single weight value and the invoice contains only one weight value with no contradictory alternative, the result is MATCH. Invoice has exactly one weight value (1.729 kg) with no alternative.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MISMATCH because '1.729' might mean 1.729 kg (decimal notation)
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: A weight of 1.729 kg is nonsensical for a new Volvo XC40 passenger car. The period is unambiguously a thousands separator. Per 4.4.3.3, the value is an integer and the separator is a period, which is a permitted thousands separator to remove.

comparison_steps.description: Search for origin indication in invoice
comparison_steps.result: Invoice explicitly states 'Country of Origin: Belgium' in the product description section. Direct, explicit match.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate origin 'Belgium' found explicitly in invoice as 'Country of Origin: Belgium'
comparison_steps.result: Exact match. No normalization needed. MATCH.

rules_applied.description: Origin identified via explicit 'Country of Origin' field in invoice
Positive verification – Belgium explicitly identified in invoice

checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate box 3: 'Belgium'. Invoice: 'Country of Origin: Belgium'. Exact textual match, no normalization required. The origin is explicitly stated in a dedicated origin field in the invoice. Belgium is an EU member state, consistent with the EU certificate type. MATCH per 4.5.1 and 4.5.5.

rule_evaluation_log.details: LC reference 'Letter of credit 120 days' found in invoice payment terms, but NOT in certificate. LC-exception requires the CERTIFICATE to contain an explicit LC reference. Not activated.
Document A is certificate, Document B is invoice. Single certificate, single invoice. Invoice number 462008187 matches certificate reference.

Criterion 1: Invoice ref 462008187 matches. Criterion 2: Consignor 'Volvo Car Corporation' found in invoice header. Criterion 3: Consignee 'Caribbean Vehicle Alliance LLC' found in invoice Customer-Payer field. All three criteria met.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Consignor – group structure / name found in invoice
rule_evaluation_log.details: 'Volvo Car Corporation' found in invoice header and footer. Direct match after case normalization.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Consignor – country verification via indirect identifiers
rule_evaluation_log.details: Sweden confirmed via footer address 'SWEDEN' and VAT prefix 'SE' in SE556074308901.
Invoice has 'Customer - Payer' field (primary invoicing field). Used as primary source.
'Caribbean Vehicle Alliance LLC' exact match after case normalization.
'Puerto Rico' matches 'PUERTO RICO' after case normalization.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Goods – generic description with invoice reference
rule_evaluation_log.details: Invoice number 462008187 and date 11.12.2025 both match exactly. Invoice content (new Volvo XC40 passenger car) is consistent with certificate description (New Volvo vehicle).
'vehicle' and 'passenger car' are equivalent per rule. Brand 'Volvo' present in both.
Invoice '1.729 kg' normalized to 1729 kg. Period is thousands separator for integer value of a car weight.
Certificate has single weight value with G/W category. Invoice has single weight value without explicit GW/NW. Point 2 applies: no contradictory alternative → MATCH.
'Country of Origin: Belgium' explicitly stated in invoice. Exact match with certificate box 3.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Samtliga kontrollpunkter överensstämmer. Avsändaren Volvo Car Corporation, mottagaren Caribbean Vehicle Alliance LLC, varubeskrivningen (nytt Volvo-fordon), kvantiteten (1729 kg bruttovikt) och ursprungslandet Belgien kan alla verifieras mot fakturan. Dokumentparet kan godkännas automatiskt.
overall_assessment.summary: All five checkpoints verified as MATCH. Consignor Volvo Car Corporation identified in invoice header with Sweden confirmed via address and VAT prefix. Consignee Caribbean Vehicle Alliance LLC matches Customer-Payer field in invoice with Puerto Rico confirmed. Goods description 'New Volvo vehicle' consistent with invoice's 'new passenger car Volvo XC40' via vehicle designation equivalence rule and verified invoice reference. Quantity G/W 1729 KGS matches invoice Weight 1.729 kg after thousands separator normalization. Country of origin Belgium explicitly confirmed in invoice.
non_blocking_observations: Certificate VIN (YV4L12UK5T2726637) and invoice Chassis No (5360726637) use different formats/numbering conventions but share trailing digits '726637'. This does not affect verification since goods are verified via invoice reference and product description match.

Certificate address (Box 24035, SE-400 22 Göteborg) differs from invoice postal address (SE-405 31 Göteborg). Per rules, address differences do not affect consignor MATCH when company name and country are verified.

Certificate consignee address (1019 Marginal J F Kennedy) differs from invoice address (Avenida Kennedy 9). Per rules, address differences do not affect consignee MATCH when company name and country are verified.

human_readable_report.title: Verifieringsrapport – Certificate of Origin mot Faktura

sections.certificate_value: Volvo car corporation, Box 24035, SE-400 22, GÖTEBORG, Sweden
sections.invoice_value: Volvo Car Corporation, SE-405 31 Göteborg, SWEDEN
sections.rule_applied: Konsignörens namn identifierat direkt i fakturans sidhuvud (koncernstruktur 4.1.0). Land verifierat via adress och moms-prefix (4.1.2.1).
sections.motivation: Certifikatets avsändare 'Volvo car corporation' återfinns i fakturans sidhuvud som 'Volvo Car Corporation' — identisk efter normalisering av versaler. Land 'Sweden' bekräftas av fakturans fotrad ('SWEDEN') och momsnummer (SE-prefix). Adressskillnad (400 22 vs 405 31) påverkar inte bedömningen.

sections.certificate_value: CARIBBEAN VEHICLE ALLIANCE LLC, 1019 MARGINAL J F KENNEDY, 00920 SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico
sections.invoice_value: Caribbean Vehicle Alliance LLC, Avenida Kennedy 9, 00920 SAN JUAN, PUERTO RICO
sections.rule_applied: Mottagarnamn och land verifierade mot fakturans Customer-Payer-fält (4.2.0.2, 4.2.2, 4.2.3). Adressskillnader påverkar ej bedömningen (4.2.4).
sections.motivation: Certifikatets mottagare 'CARIBBEAN VEHICLE ALLIANCE LLC' återfinns exakt i fakturans Customer-Payer-fält som 'Caribbean Vehicle Alliance LLC' efter versalnormalisering. Land 'Puerto Rico' överensstämmer. Gatuadress skiljer sig men detta påverkar inte bedömningen enligt reglerna.

sections.certificate_value: 1 New Volvo vehicles / 1 New Volvo vehicle, VIN number: YV4L12UK5T2726637, Invoice: 462008187, Invoice date: 11.12.2025
sections.invoice_value: 1 piece new passenger car Volvo XC40 536 L1 4B 0C1 31, Chassis No: 5360726637, Invoice No: 462008187, Date: 11.12.2025
sections.rule_applied: Generell varubeskrivning med fakturareferens (4.3.5). Fordonsbeteckningsekvivalens: 'vehicle' = 'passenger car' (4.3.3.0). Fakturanummer och datum överensstämmer exakt.
sections.motivation: Certifikatet refererar till faktura 462008187 daterad 11.12.2025 — båda matchar fakturan exakt. Certifikatets beskrivning 'New Volvo vehicle' är förenlig med fakturans '1 piece new passenger car Volvo XC40'. Beteckningarna 'vehicle' och 'passenger car' är ekvivalenta enligt specialregeln för fordon. Varumärket 'Volvo' framgår i båda dokumenten.

sections.rule_applied: Tusentalsavskiljarnormalisering (4.4.3.3): '1.729' normaliserat till 1729. Viktkategori: ensam viktuppgift utan motstridigt alternativ (4.4.2.2 punkt 2).
sections.motivation: Certifikatets kvantitet i ruta 7: 'G/W1729KGS' = bruttovikt 1729 kg. Fakturan anger 'Weight: 1.729 kg' där punkten utgör tusentalsavskiljare (1,729 kg vore orimligt för en bil). Efter normalisering: 1729 kg = 1729 KGS. Fakturan har en enda viktuppgift utan motstridigt alternativ.

sections.rule_applied: Direkt identifiering i fakturans ursprungsfält (4.5.1). Exakt överensstämmelse.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets ursprungsland 'Belgium' (ruta 3) återfinns ordagrant i fakturans fält 'Country of Origin: Belgium'. Exakt matchning utan behov av normalisering.

output_quality_checks.consistency_check_details: All 5 checkpoints are MATCH. No MISMATCH or MANUAL_REVIEW on any applicable checkpoint. comparison_result = IDENTICAL is consistent. workflow_recommendation = AUTO_APPROVAL_ELIGIBLE is consistent with IDENTICAL. All confidence values >= 0.85 (minimum is 0.92 on goods description). No inconsistency detected between motivations and statuses.
